findings.title: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.Elem
findings.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

tool_specific.issue_text: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

findings.title: python.jwt.security.jwt-hardcode.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: trailofbits.python.tarfile-extractall-traversal.tarfile-extractall-traversal
findings.description: Possible path traversal through `tarfile.open($PATH).extractall()` if the source tar is controlled by an attacker
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
findings.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08:2025 - Software or Data Integrity Failures

findings.title: javascript.jsonwebtoken.security.jwt-hardcode.hardcoded-jwt-secret
findings.description: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

findings.title: python.jwt.security.audit.jwt-exposed-data.jwt-python-exposed-data
findings.description: The object is passed strictly to jwt.encode(...) Make sure that sensitive information is not exposed through JWT token payload.

findings.title: python.jwt.security.jwt-hardcode.jwt-python-hardcoded-secret
findings.description: Hardcoded JWT secret or private key is used. This is a Insufficiently Protected Credentials weakness: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/522.html Consider using an appropriate security mechanism to protect the credentials (e.g. keeping secrets in environment variables)

findings.title: python.django.security.injection.path-traversal.path-traversal-open.path-traversal-open
findings.description: Found request data in a call to 'open'. Ensure the request data is validated or sanitized, otherwise it could result in path traversal attacks and therefore sensitive data being leaked. To mitigate, consider using os.path.abspath or os.path.realpath or the pathlib library.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: python.flask.security.injection.path-traversal-open.path-traversal-open
findings.description: Found request data in a call to 'open'. Ensure the request data is validated or sanitized, otherwise it could result in path traversal attacks.
cwe_ids: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: python.django.security.injection.ssrf.ssrf-injection-requests.ssrf-injection-requests
findings.description: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request. See https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Server_Side_Request_Forgery to learn more about SSRF vulnerabilities.

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: javascript.lang.security.audit.unsafe-formatstring.unsafe-formatstring
findings.description: Detected string concatenation with a non-literal variable in a util.format / console.log function. If an attacker injects a format specifier in the string, it will forge the log message. Try to use constant values for the format string.
cwe_ids: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: python.django.security.injection.raw-html-format.raw-html-format
findings.description: Detected user input flowing into a manually constructed HTML string. You may be accidentally bypassing secure methods of rendering HTML by manually constructing HTML and this could create a cross-site scripting vulnerability, which could let attackers steal sensitive user data. To be sure this is safe, check that the HTML is rendered safely. Otherwise, use templates (`django.shortcuts.render`) which will safely render HTML instead.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
findings.code_excerpt: message = f"Hello, {name}! Welcome to our website."

tool_specific.lines: message = f"Hello, {name}! Welcome to our website."

findings.title: python.flask.security.audit.xss.make-response-with-unknown-content.make-response-with-unknown-content
findings.description: Be careful with `flask.make_response()`. If this response is rendered onto a webpage, this could create a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability. `flask.make_response()` will not autoescape HTML. If you are rendering HTML, write your HTML in a template file and use `flask.render_template()` which will take care of escaping. If you are returning data from an API, consider using `flask.jsonify()`.
cwe_ids: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

findings.title: javascript.express.security.audit.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage.express-check-csurf-middleware-usage
findings.description: A CSRF middleware was not detected in your express application. Ensure you are either using one such as `csurf` or `csrf` (see rule references) and/or you are properly doing CSRF validation in your routes with a token or cookies.

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.pycryptodome.security.crypto-mode-without-authentication
findings.description: An encryption mode of operation is being used without proper message authentication. This can potentially result in the encrypted content to be decrypted by an attacker. Consider instead use an AEAD mode of operation like GCM.
cwe_ids: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.best-practice.unspecified-open-encoding
findings.description: Missing 'encoding' parameter. 'open()' uses device locale encodings by default, corrupting files with special characters. Specify the encoding to ensure cross-platform support when opening files in text mode (e.g. encoding="utf-8").

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.javascript.express.security.injection.tainted-sql-string
findings.description: Detected user input used to manually construct a SQL string. This is usually bad practice because manual construction could accidentally result in a SQL injection. An attacker could use a SQL injection to steal or modify contents of the database. Instead, use a parameterized query which is available by default in most database engines. Alternatively, consider using an object-relational mapper (ORM) such as Sequelize which will protect your queries.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
findings.code_excerpt: db.query(`SELECT * FROM products WHERE productName LIKE '%${name}%'`, (err, rows, fields) => {

$SQLSTR.abstract_content: SELECT * FROM products WHERE productName LIKE '%

tool_specific.lines: db.query(`SELECT * FROM products WHERE productName LIKE '%${name}%'`, (err, rows, fields) => {

findings.title: app.opengrep-rules.python.lang.security.use-defused-xml
findings.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.
cwe_ids: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
